verdict.reasoning: FixedEtherVault.withdrawAll() is protected by the noReentrant modifier, which sets the lock before the function body executes and clears it only after the body completes, preventing reentrant calls. The function also follows checks-effects-interactions by zeroing userBalances[msg.sender] before the external msg.sender.call{value: balance}() at the withdrawal logic, so there is no stale balance to exploit. The deposit() and getter functions do not perform external calls and do not introduce reentrancy risk.
